name: Senior Managers
rationale: Strategic decision-making requires human judgment and relationship management, though AI can assist with analysis and reporting.

name: Administrative & Financial Managers
rationale: Much of this role involves information processing, budgeting, and coordination tasks that AI can increasingly handle autonomously.

name: Financial Managers
rationale: Financial analysis and forecasting are highly amenable to AI augmentation, though senior judgment and stakeholder communication remain human.

name: HR Managers
rationale: Recruitment screening and HR analytics are increasingly AI-assisted, but culture, conflict resolution, and employee relations require human presence.

name: Sales & Marketing Managers
rationale: AI is transforming campaign targeting and analytics, but client relationships and creative strategy remain distinctly human.

name: Construction Managers
rationale: On-site coordination, safety decisions, and contractor management require constant physical presence and real-time judgment.

name: Restaurant Managers
rationale: Operations involve physical presence, staff management, and customer interaction; AI can assist scheduling and inventory but not the core role.

name: Accountants & Auditors
rationale: Routine bookkeeping and tax preparation are already heavily automated; auditing and compliance work faces major AI disruption.

name: Financial Advisors
rationale: Robo-advisors handle routine portfolio management, but complex financial planning and client trust relationships retain high human value.

name: Administrative Assistants
rationale: Scheduling, email management, document preparation, and data entry are all highly susceptible to AI automation.

name: HR Specialists
rationale: Resume screening, onboarding documentation, and HR analytics can be largely automated; employee relations work is more resilient.

name: Bookkeepers
rationale: Bookkeeping is almost entirely digital data entry and reconciliation — AI can already perform most of this work reliably.

name: Payroll Administrators
rationale: Payroll is a highly structured, rules-based process that is almost entirely automatable with current AI and software.

name: Insurance Agents
rationale: Quote generation and policy comparison are automated; complex claims and relationship selling retain human advantage.

name: Receptionists
rationale: Call routing, scheduling, and information lookup are increasingly handled by AI; in-person reception retains some human value.

name: Data Entry Clerks
rationale: Pure data entry is the prototypical AI-replaceable task — already largely displaced by OCR and structured data pipelines.

name: Purchasing Agents
rationale: Vendor comparison, purchase order processing, and demand forecasting are highly automatable digital tasks.

name: Software Developers
rationale: All core tasks — coding, debugging, documentation — are in AI's wheelhouse; demand may grow even as productivity per developer soars.

name: Civil Engineers
rationale: Design software and simulation are AI-enhanced, but site work, regulatory navigation, and physical judgment keep exposure moderate.

name: Electrical Engineers
rationale: Circuit design and simulation are increasingly AI-assisted; physical installation oversight and systems integration remain human.

name: Mechanical Engineers
rationale: CAD and simulation tools are AI-enhanced; hands-on prototyping, manufacturing liaison, and physical judgment provide resilience.

name: Environmental Scientists
rationale: Data analysis and modelling are AI-augmentable; field sampling, regulatory engagement, and community consultation require human presence.

name: IT Support Specialists
rationale: Tier-1 troubleshooting is increasingly handled by AI chatbots; complex infrastructure and hands-on hardware work is more resilient.

name: Database & Cloud Admins
rationale: Routine monitoring and query optimization are highly automatable; architecture decisions and incident response retain human value.

name: Cybersecurity Analysts
rationale: Threat detection is AI-powered, but strategic defence, red-teaming, and incident response require human creativity and judgment.

name: Data Scientists
rationale: Feature engineering and model selection are increasingly automated by AutoML; framing problems and communicating insights remain human.

name: Geologists
rationale: Fieldwork and sample collection are inherently physical; data interpretation and resource assessment are increasingly AI-assisted.

name: Architects
rationale: Generative design AI is rapidly transforming drafting and iteration; client engagement, site analysis, and regulatory approval are more resilient.

name: Registered Nurses
rationale: Patient assessment, clinical judgment, and therapeutic relationships are deeply human; AI assists documentation and monitoring but cannot replace bedside care.

name: Physicians & Surgeons
rationale: Diagnostic AI is advancing rapidly, but patient relationships, complex clinical reasoning, and procedural skills keep exposure moderate.

name: Pharmacists
rationale: Drug interaction checking and dispensing are automatable; patient counselling and clinical pharmacy retain significant human value.

name: Dentists
rationale: Oral examination and manual procedures require physical skill and patient presence; AI assists imaging analysis but can't replace hands-on care.

name: Personal Support Workers
rationale: Physical caregiving — bathing, dressing, mobility assistance, emotional support — requires constant human presence and cannot be automated.

name: Medical Lab Technologists
rationale: Sample analysis is increasingly automated by laboratory systems; quality control, complex cases, and instrument maintenance need human oversight.

name: Physiotherapists
rationale: Hands-on physical treatment and therapeutic relationships are central to this role; AI can assist assessment but not the physical intervention.

name: Paramedics
rationale: Emergency response in unpredictable physical environments with life-or-death time pressure is essentially immune to AI automation.

name: Dental Hygienists
rationale: Teeth cleaning and oral assessment require direct physical contact and fine motor skill; AI can assist imaging but not manual procedures.

name: Veterinarians
rationale: Animal diagnosis and surgical procedures are physical and context-dependent; AI assists with imaging and literature review.

name: Occupational Therapists
rationale: Therapeutic relationships and adaptive skill training are human-centred; AI can assist with documentation and assessment tools.

name: Elementary Teachers
rationale: AI can personalize content delivery and handle administrative tasks, but classroom management and social-emotional development require human presence.

name: Secondary Teachers
rationale: Subject matter tutoring is increasingly AI-capable, but mentoring, classroom dynamics, and curriculum design retain human importance.

name: College Instructors
rationale: Lecture content delivery can be AI-enhanced or replaced; research supervision and industry connections remain distinctly human.

name: Social Workers
rationale: Case documentation and resource matching can be AI-assisted, but relationship-based support and crisis intervention are fundamentally human.

name: Lawyers & Notaries
rationale: Legal research and document drafting are highly AI-automatable; courtroom advocacy and complex negotiations retain human advantage.

name: Early Childhood Educators
rationale: Young children's care and development requires constant, sensitive human interaction — this role is among the least exposed to AI.

name: Police Officers
rationale: Law enforcement requires physical presence, split-second judgment, and community trust; AI assists with surveillance and reporting.

name: Firefighters
rationale: Emergency response in dangerous physical environments cannot be automated; AI can assist dispatch and pre-planning only.

name: Community Service Workers
rationale: Frontline community support involves human relationship-building; case management and referral matching are increasingly AI-assisted.

name: Counsellors & Therapists
rationale: The therapeutic relationship is central to this work; AI tools can assist with session notes and assessments but not the human connection.

name: Graphic Designers
rationale: Generative image AI is already producing publication-quality graphics; creative direction and brand strategy offer some resilience.

name: Writers & Authors
rationale: LLMs can generate fluent text at scale; original voice, investigative research, and long-form narrative remain human advantages.

name: Journalists
rationale: Automated reporting handles structured data stories; investigative journalism, source relationships, and editorial judgment are more resilient.

name: Photographers & Videographers
rationale: AI image generation and editing are transforming the field; live event photography and client relationships provide some differentiation.

name: Musicians & Singers
rationale: AI music generation is advancing rapidly; live performance, artistic identity, and emotional connection to audiences provide resilience.

name: Film & TV Production
rationale: Post-production and VFX are increasingly AI-enhanced; on-set physical coordination and creative direction retain human importance.

name: Athletes & Coaches
rationale: Athletic performance and coaching require physical presence and human inspiration; AI assists analytics and training optimization only.

name: Translators & Interpreters
rationale: Neural machine translation is rapidly approaching human quality for many language pairs; specialist, legal, and simultaneous interpretation retains value.

name: Retail Salespersons
rationale: Online retail and self-checkout automate transactions; high-touch sales, luxury retail, and product expertise retain human value.

name: Food & Beverage Workers
rationale: Food preparation and service are largely physical; automation is advancing in fast food but full-service dining retains human character.

name: Cashiers
rationale: Self-checkout and automated payment are already replacing cashiers at scale; the role is in structural decline.

name: Customer Service Reps
rationale: AI chatbots handle an increasing share of customer queries; complex issue resolution and emotionally sensitive interactions retain human value.

name: Truck Drivers
rationale: Long-haul automation is advancing but regulatory and physical complexity delay deployment; last-mile and specialized transport is resilient.

name: Security Guards
rationale: Physical presence, deterrence, and incident response require human judgment; AI enhances surveillance monitoring but cannot replace guards.

name: Hotel & Hospitality Staff
rationale: Guest experience and physical service are human-centred; check-in automation and AI concierge tools are supplementing, not replacing, staff.

name: Hairstylists & Barbers
rationale: Hair services require fine manual skill, tactile judgment, and client relationships — essentially immune to AI automation.

name: Cleaners & Janitors
rationale: Physical cleaning in varied environments requires dexterity and adaptability; robotic cleaning is advancing but is far from replacing human cleaners broadly.

name: Real Estate Agents
rationale: Property search and valuation are increasingly AI-powered; negotiation, local knowledge, and client trust remain human differentiators.

name: Electricians
rationale: Wiring, installation, and fault diagnosis in varied physical environments require manual dexterity and on-site judgment; AI cannot perform physical trades.

name: Plumbers & Pipefitters
rationale: Physical pipe installation and repair in confined, unpredictable spaces is among the most robotics-resistant work in the economy.

name: Carpenters
rationale: Custom framing, finishing, and renovation work require skilled manual judgment in highly variable physical environments.

name: Welders
rationale: Industrial welding is partially automated, but custom and field welding require skill and adaptability beyond current robotics.

name: Heavy Equipment Operators
rationale: Equipment operation requires physical presence and judgment; autonomous construction equipment is emerging but not yet broadly deployed.

name: Auto Service Technicians
rationale: Diagnostic software is AI-assisted, but hands-on mechanical repair requires physical dexterity and situational judgment.

name: HVAC Technicians
rationale: Installation and repair of heating and cooling systems requires physical skill in varied environments; growing demand from electrification.

name: Industrial Electricians
rationale: Complex industrial electrical systems require expert physical troubleshooting; AI assists diagnostics but cannot perform the work.

name: Sheet Metal Workers
rationale: Fabrication and installation require precision manual skill and physical adaptability; some CNC automation is present but humans remain essential.

name: Boilermakers
rationale: Highly specialized industrial work requiring physical presence in demanding environments; AI can assist inspection but not the physical trades.

name: Farmers & Ranchers
rationale: Precision agriculture and drone technology are increasing AI involvement, but physical land management remains human-dependent.

name: Fishing Industry Workers
rationale: Physically demanding marine work with high variability; some vessel automation is present but the work is largely human.

name: Forestry Workers
rationale: Harvesting and silviculture in varied terrain require physical skill; some harvesting equipment is increasingly automated.

name: Mining Workers & Supervisors
rationale: Remote and autonomous mining equipment is advancing, but underground operations and specialized extraction remain human-driven.

name: Oil & Gas Workers
rationale: Drilling and extraction operations involve increasing automation and AI monitoring, but physical field work and safety oversight remain human.

name: Landscapers & Groundskeepers
rationale: Physical outdoor work with high variability — mowing, planting, pruning — is among the least susceptible to current AI automation.

name: Industrial Machine Operators
rationale: Many machine operation tasks are being automated by programmable CNC and robotic systems; monitoring and maintenance roles persist.

name: Assemblers
rationale: Repetitive assembly is a primary target for industrial automation; robotic assembly lines are displacing workers in manufacturing.

name: Quality Inspectors
rationale: Computer vision AI is increasingly able to detect defects more reliably than human inspectors; this role faces significant automation pressure.

name: Food Processing Workers
rationale: Some processing steps are automatable but food handling variability, seasonal work, and hygiene requirements slow full automation.

name: Textile Workers
rationale: Highly repetitive textile operations are well-suited to automation; the Canadian textile sector is in long-term structural decline.

name: Utilities Workers
rationale: Grid monitoring and fault detection are increasingly AI-assisted; physical infrastructure maintenance and emergency response require human workers.

name: Print & Publishing Operators
rationale: Digital publishing and AI-assisted layout have sharply reduced demand for print operators; the sector continues to contract.
